page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 28 physical cores and 56 vCPUs via hyperthreading, operating with a dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 448.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is highly polarized; the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 10%, and small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is in the top 25%, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the middle 50%. Computational tasks such as software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing demonstrate strong performance, while text processing and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier.

With its high memory density and strong multi-core performance, this instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, where PassMark database benchmarks show strong top-25% performance. It is also capable of handling medium-scale LLM inference, showing strong text generation speeds for 7B parameter models. The absence of local NVMe storage and GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for tasks requiring high-speed local disk I/O or heavy parallel graphics acceleration. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides a dense allocation of RAM and dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, offering a balanced profile for enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity without the added cost of specialized accelerator hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 is a memory-optimized server instance configured with 56 vCPUs and 448.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with 28 physical cores and dedicated CPU allocation, it delivers 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU. Benchmarks show strong top-25% performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and database operations, alongside a top-10% PassMark memory score. However, large-block uncached memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance represents a specialized tradeoff, prioritizing high memory capacity and multi-threaded compute. It is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, database operations, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 is a memory-optimized instance with 56 vCPUs, 448 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong multi-core and top-tier memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-56 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 56 vCPUs, 448 GB RAM, and dedicated AMD EPYC processors.
tagline: A memory-dense, hyperthreaded AMD EPYC server delivering strong multi-core compute and top-tier memory performance for enterprise databases and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 56 vCPUs and 28 physical cores.
Equipped with 448.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Top 10% PassMark memory score and top 25% multi-core CPU performance.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration.
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and multi-threaded software compilation.
